concepts.label: Host Environments
concepts.description: The runtime container that embeds a JS engine and provides platform-specific APIs (browser: DOM/fetch; Node.js: fs/http).

concepts.label: JavaScript Engine
concepts.description: The component (e.g. V8) that implements ECMAScript and executes JavaScript code.

concepts.label: Type Erasure
concepts.description: TypeScript types are stripped at compile time; nothing type-related exists at runtime.

concepts.label: var Hoisting
concepts.description: var declarations are hoisted and initialized to undefined; accessing them before assignment returns undefined, not an error.

concepts.label: Temporal Dead Zone
concepts.description: The period between entering a block scope and a let/const declaration; accessing the variable throws ReferenceError.

concepts.label: Block Scope
concepts.description: let and const are scoped to the nearest enclosing block {}, unlike var which is function-scoped.

concepts.label: Primitive Types
concepts.description: The 8 JavaScript primitives: string, number, bigint, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, object.

concepts.label: typeof Operator
concepts.description: Returns a string indicating the type of an operand; notable quirk: typeof null === 'object'.

concepts.label: Type Coercion
concepts.description: Implicit conversion between types via ==, + with strings, boolean contexts, and arithmetic operators.

concepts.label: Strict Equality (===)
concepts.description: === checks type and value without coercion. Always use === over == to avoid implicit conversion bugs.

concepts.label: Array Methods
concepts.description: map, filter, reduce, find, findIndex, some, every, flat, flatMap — functional array transformation patterns.

concepts.label: Spread & Rest
concepts.description: ... spread expands iterables into function args or array literals; ... rest collects remaining args into an array.

concepts.label: Destructuring
concepts.description: Unpack values from arrays or properties from objects into variables, with rename and default syntax.

concepts.label: Object Spread
concepts.description: { ...obj } creates a shallow copy; later properties override earlier ones — the basis for immutable updates.

concepts.label: Function Forms
concepts.description: Function declarations vs expressions vs arrow functions — hoisting, this, and appropriate use cases.

concepts.label: Hoisting
concepts.description: The JavaScript behavior of moving declarations (var, function) to the top of their scope before execution.

concepts.label: Lexical Scope
concepts.description: Scope is determined at write-time from the source position of code, not at call-time.

concepts.label: Closures
concepts.description: Functions that retain access to variables from their lexical scope after that scope has exited.

concepts.label: Loop Closure Bug
concepts.description: Using var in a loop creates one shared binding; all closures capture the same variable. Fix with let.

concepts.label: this Binding
concepts.description: The value of this is determined by the call site, not the function definition — five binding rules apply.

concepts.label: this Call-Site Rules
concepts.description: Priority order: new > explicit (.call/.apply/.bind) > implicit (obj.method()) > default (undefined/global).

concepts.label: Explicit Binding
concepts.description: call(), apply(), and bind() explicitly set the this value. bind() returns a new function; the others invoke immediately.

concepts.label: Arrow Functions
concepts.description: Arrow functions have no own this — they inherit it lexically from the enclosing scope. Cannot be overridden with call/bind.

concepts.label: Event Loop
concepts.description: The mechanism that picks tasks from the task queue and runs them on the call stack one at a time.

concepts.label: Microtask Queue
concepts.description: Promise callbacks queue as microtasks, which drain entirely after each task before the next macrotask runs.

concepts.label: Promises
concepts.description: Represent the eventual result of an async operation; states: pending, fulfilled, rejected.

concepts.label: Promise Combinators
concepts.description: Promise.all (fail-fast parallel), Promise.allSettled (wait-all parallel), Promise.race, Promise.any.

concepts.label: async / await
concepts.description: Syntactic sugar over Promises. async functions always return a Promise; await pauses until settlement.

concepts.label: ES Modules
concepts.description: The standard import/export module system. Static, tree-shakeable, works in browser and Node.js.

concepts.label: CommonJS vs ESM
concepts.description: CJS uses require/module.exports (dynamic, Node.js legacy). ESM uses import/export (static, modern). They are not directly interoperable.

concepts.label: tsconfig.json
concepts.description: TypeScript compiler configuration: strict, target, lib, module, moduleResolution, noEmit.

concepts.label: Type Inference
concepts.description: TypeScript infers types from context — literal types for const, widened types for let, parameter types must be explicit.

concepts.label: Union Types
concepts.description: string | number — a type that can be one of several types. Requires handling all members for safe use.

concepts.label: strictNullChecks
concepts.description: With strictNullChecks, null and undefined are separate types. Accessing properties on nullable types requires a guard.

concepts.label: Literal Types
concepts.description: Types that accept only specific values: 'pending' | 'complete' | 'failed'. Used for type-safe string constants.

concepts.label: Structural Typing
concepts.description: Type compatibility is based on shape, not name. A type that has at least the required properties is compatible.

concepts.label: Interface vs Type Alias
concepts.description: interface: object shapes, supports extends and declaration merging. type: unions, intersections, utility type expressions.

concepts.label: Generics
concepts.description: Type parameters <T> that are filled in at use-site, enabling type-safe reusable functions and classes.

concepts.label: keyof Operator
concepts.description: keyof T produces a union of T's property names as string literals. Used with generics for type-safe property access.

concepts.label: Utility Types
concepts.description: Built-in type transformers: Partial, Required, Readonly, Record, Pick, Omit, NonNullable, ReturnType, Parameters.

concepts.label: Type Narrowing
concepts.description: TypeScript refines a type within a conditional block based on runtime checks: typeof, instanceof, in, truthiness.

concepts.label: Discriminated Unions
concepts.description: Union types with a shared literal-typed property (discriminant) that enables exhaustive switch/if narrowing.

concepts.label: DOM Model
concepts.description: The tree of nodes representing an HTML document; manipulated via createElement, appendChild, textContent, classList.

concepts.label: DOM Query Selectors
concepts.description: querySelector, querySelectorAll, getElementById — selecting elements with CSS selectors or IDs.

concepts.label: DOM Events
concepts.description: addEventListener, event object, bubbling, stopPropagation, CustomEvent, and removing listeners.

concepts.label: Event Delegation
concepts.description: Attaching one listener to a parent to handle events from children via bubbling — efficient and works for dynamic children.

concepts.label: Node.js Runtime
concepts.description: V8 outside the browser with process, Buffer, and built-in modules (fs, path, os, child_process).

concepts.label: Node.js Built-in Modules
concepts.description: node:fs, node:path, node:url, node:os — core modules available without installation.

concepts.label: Node.js HTTP Server
concepts.description: http.createServer, request/response streams, routing, body parsing, and res.end().

concepts.label: JavaScript History
concepts.description: Created by Brendan Eich in 10 days (1995), Netscape Navigator, standardized as ECMAScript by ECMA International. Went from toy script language to the world's most deployed runtime.

concepts.label: ECMAScript & TC39
concepts.description: ECMAScript is the language spec; TC39 is the committee that evolves it. Yearly releases since ES2015 (ES6). Engines (V8, SpiderMonkey) implement the spec.

concepts.label: TypeScript Origin
concepts.description: Created by Anders Hejlsberg at Microsoft (2012). TypeScript adds a static type system on top of JavaScript to catch bugs at compile time, then erases types to plain JS.

concepts.label: JS Strengths & Weaknesses
concepts.description: Strengths: ubiquitous (browser + server), excellent async model, huge ecosystem. Weaknesses: type coercion gotchas, this binding complexity, legacy baggage from the 10-day origin.
